upper_intermediate.title: Needn't, Don't Need To, Didn't Need To, Needn't Have
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Needn't / Don't need to – Hiện tại: Không cần thiết phải làm
Cả hai đều diễn đạt sự vắng mặt của sự cần thiết ở hiện tại hoặc tương lai:
You needn't come early. The meeting starts at 10.
You don't need to come early. The meeting starts at 10.

⚠️ needn't = modal verb → needn't + bare infinitive (KHÔNG có "to")
⚠️ don't need to = ordinary verb → don't/doesn't need + to-infinitive

needn't thường dùng trong tiếng Anh Anh, văn phong trang trọng hơn, thường để cho phép ai đó KHÔNG làm gì:
You needn't apologise. It wasn't your fault.
She doesn't need to work this weekend. Her boss said it's fine.
📌 Didn't need to vs Needn't have – Quá khứ
Đây là điểm QUAN TRỌNG dễ nhầm lẫn nhất:

1. didn't need to + infinitive → Không cần làm, và đã KHÔNG làm (hoặc không rõ):
He started to feel better, so I didn't need to call the doctor. (= Tôi đã không gọi vì không cần.)
The exam was cancelled, so she didn't need to study.

2. needn't have + past participle → Đã làm, nhưng thực ra KHÔNG CẦN làm – lãng phí công sức:
You needn't have cooked so much food. We're only three people! (= Bạn đã nấu rồi, nhưng không cần vậy.)
She needn't have bought a new dress. She already had a perfect one.

⚠️ Khi hành động đã xảy ra và là không cần thiết, cả hai cách đều có thể dùng:
You didn't need to buy / needn't have bought dessert. (= You did buy it.)

⚠️ Khi hành động KHÔNG xảy ra, chỉ dùng didn't need to:
I didn't need to buy groceries because Sarah had already done it. ✔
I needn't have bought groceries… ✘ (không đúng nếu bạn không mua)
⚠️ Tóm tắt so sánh
DạngNghĩaĐã làm chưa?
needn't / don't need toKhông cần phải làm (hiện tại/tương lai)Chưa làm
didn't need toKhông cần làm (quá khứ, thường không làm)Thường không làm
needn't have doneĐã làm, nhưng không cần thiếtĐÃ làm rồi
You needn't wait for me. I'll take a taxi.Không cần thiết → needn't + bare infinitive
She needn't have ironed all those shirts – we're going to the beach!Đã là rồi nhưng không cần → needn't have + past participle
I didn't need to wake up early because the meeting was cancelled.Không cần → đã không làm → didn't need to

exercises.q: You ___ buy a new one. I can lend you mine.
exercises.exp: Không cần mua vì có thể mượn → needn't + bare infinitive

exercises.q: She ___ have prepared so many snacks. There were only five guests!
exercises.exp: Đã chuẩn bị rồi nhưng không cần → needn't have + past participle

exercises.q: The test was postponed, so the students ___ stay up all night studying.
exercises.exp: Không cần làm → thường không làm → didn't need to

exercises.q: You ___ whisper. Nobody else is in the house.
exercises.exp: Không cần thì thầm vì không có ai → don't need to

exercises.q: He ___ have driven all the way here. We could have done the meeting online.
exercises.exp: Đã lái xe đến rồi, nhưng không cần thiết → needn't have + past participle

exercises.q: I ___ wait because my friend arrived exactly on time.
exercises.exp: Không phải chờ vì bạn đúng giờ → didn't need to (không chờ)

exercises.q: ___ you ___ wear a uniform at your new school?
exercises.exp: Hỏi về sự cần thiết ở hiện tại → do/does + need to

exercises.q: You ___ have bought flowers – but thank you, they're lovely!
exercises.exp: Đã mua hoa rồi, không cần thiết nhưng vẫn cảm ơn → needn't have

exercises.q: The instructions are very clear. You ___ ask for help.
exercises.exp: Hướng dẫn rõ ràng → không cần hỏi → needn't

exercises.q: She ___ have worried so much about the interview. She got the job easily.
exercises.exp: Đã lo lắng rồi, nhưng thực ra không cần → needn't have worried

exercises.q: The doctor said I ___ take the full course of antibiotics.
exercises.exp: Bác sĩ nói cần uống đủ liều → need to (cần thiết)

exercises.q: We had a spare key, so the locksmith ___ come.
exercises.exp: Không cần thợ khóa → và đã không gọi → didn't need to

exercises.q: You ___ have told me about the party. I already knew!
exercises.exp: Đã nói rồi, nhưng tôi đã biết → needn't have told

exercises.q: He ___ take the bus. His friend is giving him a lift.
exercises.exp: Không cần đi xe buýt vì có người đưa → doesn't need to

exercises.q: I rushed to the airport but the flight was delayed. I ___ have hurried at all.
exercises.exp: Đã vội rồi nhưng vô ích → needn't have hurried

exercises.q: Students ___ wear formal clothes to class, but smart casual is recommended.
exercises.exp: Không bắt buộc mặc trang trọng → don't need to

exercises.q: She ___ have printed all those pages. The professor said it was fine to submit online.
exercises.exp: Đã in rồi, nhưng không cần → needn't have printed

exercises.q: I found out the event was free, so I ___ buy a ticket in advance.
exercises.exp: Sự kiện miễn phí → không cần mua, đã không mua → didn't need to

exercises.q: You ___ mention this to anyone. It's confidential.
exercises.exp: Cấm tiết lộ → mustn't (prohibition, not just lack of necessity)

exercises.q: The manager said the report ___ be submitted until next Friday.
exercises.exp: Không cần nộp sớm → doesn't need to

exercises.q: You ___ (not/come) all this way. We could have talked on the phone!
exercises.exp: Đã đến rồi nhưng không cần thiết → needn't have + past participle

exercises.q: The concert was free, so we ___ (not/pay) for tickets.
exercises.exp: Miễn phí → không cần trả → đã không trả → didn't need to

exercises.q: She ___ (not/worry) so much – everything turned out perfectly.
exercises.exp: Đã lo lắng rồi, nhưng vô ích → needn't have worried

exercises.q: You ___ (not/knock) – the door is always open.
exercises.ans: needn't knock / don't need to knock
exercises.exp: Không cần gõ cửa → needn't hoặc don't need to

exercises.q: I found the keys on my desk, so I ___ (not/call) the locksmith.
exercises.exp: Tìm thấy chìa khóa → không cần gọi → đã không gọi

exercises.q: He ___ (not/bring) a gift, but it was very kind of him.
exercises.ans: needn't have brought / didn't need to bring
exercises.exp: Đã mang quà, không cần thiết → needn't have; hoặc didn't need to

exercises.q: The meeting was moved to next week, so she ___ (not/prepare) the presentation today.
exercises.exp: Không cần chuẩn bị → đã không chuẩn bị → didn't need to

exercises.q: You ___ (not/apologise). It really wasn't your fault.
exercises.ans: needn't apologise / don't need to apologise
exercises.exp: Không cần xin lỗi → needn't + bare inf

exercises.q: I spent three hours cleaning the house for nothing. My guests cancelled. I ___ (not/clean) at all!
exercises.exp: Đã dọn dẹp rồi nhưng hoàn toàn không cần → needn't have cleaned

exercises.q: He ___ (not/wear) a tie to the interview. It was a casual company.
exercises.ans: didn't need to wear / needn't have worn
exercises.exp: Công ty casual → không cần thắt cà vạt

exercises.q: You ___ (not/shout). I'm standing right next to you.
exercises.ans: needn't shout / don't need to shout
exercises.exp: Đứng gần → không cần hét

exercises.q: We bought extra supplies, but the weather was terrible and we ___ (not/go) camping at all.
exercises.exp: Không đi vì thời tiết xấu → didn't need to go

exercises.q: The test was postponed, so you ___ (not/study) all night.
exercises.exp: Thi hoãn → không cần học → đã không học

exercises.q: I ___ (not/buy) the tickets because my friend already had spares.
exercises.exp: Bạn đã có vé → không cần mua → đã không mua

exercises.q: The concert was free, so we ___ (not/pay) for tickets. We just walked in.
exercises.exp: Miễn phí → không cần trả → đã không trả

exercises.q: You ___ (not/print) the boarding pass. I can show it on my phone.
exercises.ans: don't need to print / needn't print
exercises.exp: Có trên điện thoại → không cần in

exercises.q: He found the keys immediately, so he ___ (not/call) a locksmith at all.
exercises.exp: Tìm thấy chìa khóa → không cần gọi thợ khóa

exercises.q: The report was cancelled, so she ___ (not/write) the full version.
exercises.exp: Báo cáo bị hủy → không cần viết phiên bản đầy đủ

exercises.q: I ___ (not/spend) so much time on the assignment. It turned out to be optional.
exercises.ans: needn't have spent / didn't need to spend
exercises.exp: Bài tập không bắt buộc → không cần dành nhiều thời gian

exercises.q: You ___ (not/be) so hard on yourself. You did your best under the circumstances.
exercises.ans: needn't have been / didn't need to be
exercises.exp: Đã tự trách mình nhưng không cần thiết

passage.text: Last weekend, my sister organised a surprise birthday party for me. She {{1}} spent days preparing every detail. She {{2}} made three different cakes because there were only twelve guests, and she {{3}} decorated the entire garden since the weather was perfect – she {{4}} just opened the windows and let the sunlight in. She {{5}} contacted every single person individually; a group message would have been enough. On the day, I told her I already knew about the party – a friend had let it slip – so she {{6}} kept it a secret so carefully. She {{7}} have gone to all that trouble. But her efforts were so touching that I pretended to be surprised anyway. In the future, she {{8}} worry so much. I am just happy when people remember. The truth is, she {{9}} have done anything special – her presence alone would have been enough. Still, I will never forget the effort she {{10}} into making that evening memorable.

When I moved to a new city for work, I panicked and overprepared for everything. I {{1}} bought six months of supplies on my first day – I {{2}} just bought things as I needed them. I {{3}} hired a moving company for every single item; I {{4}} brought the smaller things myself. I also {{5}} signed up for every social app in town trying to make friends – a colleague told me later that there was a welcoming group for new employees and I {{6}} signed up for anything else at all. I {{7}} worried so much about being lonely; everyone in the office was incredibly friendly. I {{8}} have stressed so much in the first week. These days, I realise that when you move somewhere new, you {{9}} plan every single detail in advance. You {{10}} be so anxious – things tend to fall into place.

Tom was preparing for his driving test and was convinced he would fail. He {{1}} practised the same route twelve times in one week – his instructor told him he {{2}} repeated it more than four or five times. He {{3}} memorised every single road sign; the examiner only checks a few. He also {{4}} asked his parents to accompany him, as candidates {{5}} bring anyone with them. On the morning of the test, he arrived an hour early, which meant he {{6}} waited in the car park alone, feeling even more nervous. His mother told him he {{7}} panicked – he had been driving perfectly for weeks. In the end, he passed first time. He {{8}} worried so much after all. Now he says that next time he helps a friend prepare for a test, he {{9}} let them over-prepare. A calm, well-rested mind is all you {{10}} bring to an exam.

The company realised it had been wasting resources for years. Employees {{1}} submitted weekly paper reports – the digital system had been in place all along and everyone {{2}} simply uploaded a file. The IT department {{3}} ordered new computers for every desk because most staff worked primarily from laptops. The catering team {{4}} prepared hot lunches daily, as surveys showed most people preferred to bring their own food. The management team {{5}} held three-hour meetings every Monday; a fifteen-minute call would have been enough. The HR department {{6}} printed welcome packs for new staff when everything {{7}} have been sent by email. The CEO admitted that the company {{8}} spent so heavily on office space since the shift to remote working had made it redundant. Going forward, they {{9}} reconsider all these practices. The board agreed that a culture of efficiency {{10}} replace the old habit of doing more than necessary.

Ana had been dreading her performance review for weeks. She {{1}} prepared a forty-slide presentation – her manager later said a brief summary would have been enough. She {{2}} stayed at the office until midnight the night before; the review was informal and her manager {{3}} even looked at any documents. She {{4}} brought printed copies of every project because her manager had access to everything online. In the meeting, her manager said she {{5}} worried at all – her performance had been outstanding. She also said that Ana {{6}} have requested a formal conference room; a chat over coffee would have been perfectly fine. Ana felt relieved. She realised she {{7}} put herself under such pressure. In the future, she {{8}} remind herself that good work speaks for itself. She {{9}} ever doubt her value again, and she {{10}} have lost so much sleep over something that went so well.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một lần bạn đã chuẩn bị quá kỹ cho điều gì đó mà hoàn toàn không cần thiết. Điều gì đã xảy ra, bạn đã làm gì, và bạn học được gì từ trải nghiệm đó?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: needn't have + past participle, didn't need to, needn't + bare infinitive
writing_prompts.example: Last year, I spent an entire weekend preparing for a job interview that turned out to be extremely casual. I needn't have printed fifteen copies of my CV – the interviewer told me straightaway that everything was already on the system. I didn't need to memorise the company's entire history, and I needn't have bought a new suit since everyone in the office was in jeans and T-shirts. I had also practised my answers for four hours with a friend, but the conversation was so relaxed that I needn't have rehearsed anything formal at all. The interviewer laughed and said I needn't have been so nervous. Looking back, I wasted a whole weekend that I could have spent resting. I've since learnt that you don't need to over-prepare for everything – sometimes being natural and well-rested is more effective than any amount of rehearsal.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một quy tắc hoặc thủ tục tại trường học, nơi làm việc, hoặc trong gia đình bạn mà bạn cho là không cần thiết. Tại sao nó không cần thiết và bạn sẽ thay đổi điều gì?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: don't need to, needn't, needn't have, didn't need to
writing_prompts.example: At my previous workplace, every employee had to attend a two-hour briefing every Monday morning. In my opinion, we didn't need to be there in person at all – a short email summary would have been perfectly sufficient. The managers needn't have prepared elaborate slide presentations for each meeting because most of the information was already available on the company intranet. We also didn't need to fill in a separate attendance form at the start of every meeting, as our schedules were already logged digitally. I felt that the company needn't have maintained this ritual simply out of tradition. If I could change things, I would suggest that staff don't need to meet face-to-face more than once a month, and that brief written updates would save everyone a great deal of time and energy.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy nói về một tình huống mà bạn hoặc ai đó bạn biết đã lo lắng rất nhiều về điều gì đó, nhưng cuối cùng mọi chuyện đều ổn. Sử dụng needn't have và các cấu trúc liên quan.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: needn't have, didn't need to, don't need to + reflection on over-anxiety
writing_prompts.example: When my best friend was waiting for her university results last summer, she was absolutely convinced she had failed. She needn't have panicked so much – she had been consistently strong throughout the year. She didn't need to reread all her notes the night before the results came out, but she stayed up until three in the morning anyway. She needn't have called me in tears at midnight, though I was glad she did. When the results arrived, she had passed with distinction. She needn't have spent weeks imagining failure when she had clearly done the work. She told me afterwards that she realised she didn't need to measure herself by her worst fears – she should measure herself by the effort she had actually put in. I think we all needn't be so quick to assume the worst.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một khoảng thời gian bạn đã lãng phí tiền bạc hoặc công sức vào điều gì đó không cần thiết. Điều gì đã xảy ra, bạn đã nhận ra điều gì, và bạn sẽ làm gì khác nếu có cơ hội?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: needn't have + past participle, didn't need to, don't need to
writing_prompts.example: Last summer, I needn't have booked an expensive hotel for my weekend trip to the mountains. When I arrived, I discovered that my friend's family had a cabin nearby, and they had already invited me to stay with them for free. I didn't need to spend three hundred dollars on accommodation when I had a perfectly comfortable place to stay without paying anything at all. I needn't have worried about the budget beforehand either, because the whole trip ended up being much cheaper than I had planned. I also needn't have packed so many clothes – I barely changed outfits the entire time. Looking back, I realise that I needn't have stressed about money for a trip where most expenses turned out to be unnecessary. I've since learned that when you travel with generous friends, you don't need to plan every detail in advance.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một quy định hoặc thói quen xã hội mà bạn nghĩ là không cần thiết hoặc lỗi thời. Giải thích tại sao nó tồn tại, tại sao nó không cần thiết, và bạn nghĩ nên thay đổi như thế nào.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: don't need to, needn't, needn't have, didn't need to
writing_prompts.example: In my home country, there is an old tradition that requires guests to bring a gift whenever they visit someone's home. In my opinion, this is a tradition that we don't need to maintain in modern times. Hosts needn't feel obligated to offer elaborate meals anymore, and guests needn't spend money on things that the host might not even want or need. A simple phone call to say hello would be perfectly sufficient. I didn't need to grow up feeling guilty whenever I visited relatives without bringing something, because the real purpose of visiting should be about connection, not exchange. There is no need to create social pressure around gift-giving. I believe we could simply redefine what it means to be a good guest – being present, engaged, and attentive is more valuable than any physical gift. Perhaps we don't need to abandon the tradition entirely, but we certainly needn't treat it as an obligation.

upper_intermediate.title: Past Modal Verbs of Deduction
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Modal verbs of deduction – Ôn lại hiện tại
Chúng ta dùng modal verbs để suy luận về điều gì đó dựa trên bằng chứng hiện có:
ModalMức độ chắc chắnVí dụ
must beGần như chắc chắn (positive)You must be tired after such a long journey.
can't/couldn't beGần như chắc chắn (negative)That can't be John – he's abroad.
might/may/could beCó thể (không chắc)She might be at the library.
📌 Past Modals of Deduction – Suy luận về quá khứ
Để suy luận về những gì đã xảy ra trong quá khứ, chúng ta dùng modal + have + past participle:

must have + past participle → Khá chắc chắn điều gì đó ĐÃ xảy ra:
I can't find my wallet. I must have dropped it in the taxi.
You must have been exhausted after running the marathon.

can't have / couldn't have + past participle → Khá chắc chắn điều gì đó ĐÃ KHÔNG xảy ra:
She can't have passed the exam – she didn't even study.
You couldn't have seen John last night. He was in hospital.

might have / may have / could have + past participle → Có thể đã xảy ra (không chắc):
I think I might have left my phone in the restaurant.
She may have missed the last train.
He could have gone home early without telling anyone.
⚠️ Bảng tổng hợp: Suy luận về quá khứ
Cấu trúcNghĩa
must have doneChắc chắn đã làm (positive deduction)
can't / couldn't have doneChắc chắn không thể đã làm (negative deduction)
might / may / could have doneCó thể đã làm (uncertain possibility)

⚠️ Lưu ý: mustn't have KHÔNG dùng để suy luận. Dùng can't have hoặc couldn't have thay thế.
⚠️ could have done cũng có thể có nghĩa là "đáng lẽ có thể làm" (unrealised ability) – ngữ cảnh sẽ phân biệt.
The door is open. Someone must have forgotten to lock it.Chắc chắn đã quên → must have + past participle
She can't have taken the money – she wasn't even here.Không thể đã lấy (không có mặt) → can't have
He's not answering. He might have fallen asleep.Có thể đã ngủ → might have + past participle

exercises.q: The lights are off and nobody answered the door. They ___ gone out.
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,might not have,should have
exercises.exp: Bằng chứng rõ → gần như chắc chắn đã đi ra ngoài → must have

exercises.q: You ___ eaten the whole cake! You said you weren't hungry.
exercises.exp: Vô lý/không thể (vừa nói không đói) → can't have eaten

exercises.q: I'm not sure where Sam is. He ___ gone to the library.
exercises.exp: Không chắc chắn → có thể đã đến thư viện → might have

exercises.q: She looked so tired this morning. She ___ been up all night.
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,might not have,needn't have
exercises.exp: Trông rất mệt → suy luận chắc chắn → must have been

exercises.q: That ___ been John at the party last night. He's been in Tokyo all week.
exercises.exp: Đang ở Tokyo → không thể ở đó → can't have been

exercises.q: The thief ___ had a key. There was no sign of a break-in.
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,might not have,needn't have
exercises.exp: Không có dấu hiệu cạy phá → chắc chắn đã có chìa → must have had

exercises.q: I left my bag on the train. Someone ___ handed it in to lost property.
exercises.exp: Không chắc → có thể đã nộp vào nơi lưu giữ đồ thất lạc → might have

exercises.q: She passed every test with perfect scores. She ___ studied very hard.
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,might not have,needn't have
exercises.exp: Kết quả hoàn hảo → suy ra đã học rất chăm → must have studied

exercises.q: They ___ received my email. It says 'delivered' but they haven't replied.
exercises.opts: might not have,must have,can't have,needn't have
exercises.exp: Đã giao nhưng chưa trả lời → có thể không đọc → might not have

exercises.q: The window is broken. The ball ___ hit it when the kids were playing.
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,might not have,needn't have
exercises.exp: Kính vỡ + bọn trẻ đang chơi → chắc chắn do bóng → must have hit

exercises.q: He ___ arrived at the airport yet. He only left the house twenty minutes ago.
exercises.exp: Mới ra đi 20 phút → không thể đến sân bay rồi → can't have arrived

exercises.q: There's smoke coming from the kitchen. Someone ___ left something on the stove.
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,needn't have,might not have
exercises.exp: Có khói → suy ra chắc chắn ai đó để quên → must have left

exercises.q: She doesn't look upset at all. She ___ heard the bad news yet.
exercises.exp: Không có vẻ buồn → chắc chưa nghe → can't have heard

exercises.q: I think I ___ left my phone at the café. I can't find it anywhere.
exercises.exp: Không chắc → có thể đã bỏ quên → might have left

exercises.q: You ___ worked for the same company for thirty years without a single complaint!
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,might not have,needn't have
exercises.exp: Suy luận dựa trên sự ngưỡng mộ → must have worked

exercises.q: The roads are completely dry. It ___ rained last night.
exercises.exp: Đường khô hoàn toàn → không thể mưa đêm qua → can't have rained

exercises.q: He failed the exam. He ___ understood the topic very well.
exercises.exp: Trượt thi → không thể đã hiểu bài → can't have understood

exercises.q: She ___ taken a different route. Her car isn't in the car park.
exercises.exp: Xe không thấy → có thể đi đường khác → might have taken

exercises.q: He was sweating and out of breath. He ___ been running.
exercises.opts: must have,can't have,needn't have,might not have
exercises.exp: Đổ mồ hôi + thở hổn hển → chắc chắn đã chạy → must have been running

exercises.q: Nobody saw the accident. It ___ happened in the early hours of the morning.
exercises.exp: Không ai chứng kiến → suy ra xảy ra lúc sáng sớm → must have happened

exercises.q: The front door is wide open. Someone ___ (forget) to lock it.
exercises.exp: Cửa mở → chắc chắn ai đó quên khóa → must have forgotten

exercises.q: She ___ (study) all night. Her eyes are red and she can barely stay awake.
exercises.exp: Mắt đỏ, buồn ngủ → chắc chắn đã thức học → must have been studying

exercises.q: That ___ (be) their car in the driveway. They sold it last month.
exercises.exp: Đã bán tháng trước → không thể là xe của họ → can't have been

exercises.q: He's not answering his phone. He ___ (leave) it at home.
exercises.ans: might have left / may have left / could have left
exercises.exp: Không chắc → có thể bỏ quên điện thoại ở nhà

exercises.q: You ___ (be) terrified when you heard the explosion!
exercises.exp: Nghe tiếng nổ → chắc chắn đã hoảng sợ → must have been

exercises.q: She ___ (pass) the test. She doesn't know any grammar at all.
exercises.exp: Không biết ngữ pháp → không thể đã qua → can't have passed

exercises.q: I can't find my glasses anywhere. I ___ (leave) them at the restaurant.
exercises.ans: might have left / may have left / could have left
exercises.exp: Không chắc chắn nơi để quên → might/may/could have left

exercises.q: The match ended 5–0. The home team ___ (play) brilliantly.
exercises.exp: Tỷ số áp đảo → suy ra đội chủ nhà chơi rất hay

exercises.q: He ___ (see) the accident. He was standing right there.
exercises.exp: Đứng ngay đó → chắc chắn đã thấy → must have seen

exercises.q: The food is all gone. The children ___ (eat) everything.
exercises.exp: Hết thức ăn → suy ra lũ trẻ đã ăn hết

exercises.q: No one answered. They ___ (go) to bed already – it's almost midnight.
exercises.ans: must have gone / might have gone
exercises.exp: Gần nửa đêm, không ai trả lời → có thể đã ngủ

exercises.q: The car is gone from the driveway. They ___ (leave) early this morning.
exercises.exp: Xe đi rồi → chắc chắn đã đi sớm

exercises.q: She didn't come to the party. She ___ (be) busy with work.
exercises.exp: Không đến → chắc chắn đã bận việc

exercises.q: The milk smells sour. It ___ (be) left out of the fridge overnight.
exercises.exp: Sữa chua → chắc chắn để ngoài tủ lạnh

exercises.q: He looks so refreshed. He ___ (have) a good night's sleep last night.
exercises.exp: Trông tươi tắn → chắc chắn ngủ ngon

exercises.q: The shop is closed. They ___ (go) on vacation – the sign says 'Back in two weeks'.
exercises.exp: Cửa hàng đóng + biển thông báo → chắc chắn đi nghỉ

exercises.q: He failed all his exams. He ___ (study) at all during the semester.
exercises.exp: Trượt hết → không thể đã học

exercises.q: The floor is wet. Someone ___ (spill) water on it recently.
exercises.exp: Sàn ướt → chắc chắn ai đó làm đổ nước

exercises.q: She looks extremely happy. She ___ (hear) the good news about her promotion.
exercises.exp: Trông vui → chắc chắn đã nghe tin tốt

exercises.q: The package hasn't arrived yet. There ___ (be) a delay in shipping.
exercises.exp: Chưa đến → chắc chắn có trì hoãn

you must have been very relieved when they said everything was fine

passage.text: The police were investigating a burglary at a local jewellery shop. The front door was undamaged, which meant the burglar {{1}} used a key or known the security code. There were muddy footprints on the floor – the burglar {{2}} entered through the back garden. The alarm had not been triggered, so the burglar {{3}} known how to disable it. A witness reported seeing a black van parked outside at midnight. The driver {{4}} been waiting while someone else went inside. The safe was missing and was very heavy, so the burglar {{5}} worked alone – it {{6}} taken at least two people to carry it. The security camera had been switched off, which meant someone {{7}} planned the whole thing in advance. A member of staff {{8}} given inside information, as only employees knew the camera schedule. A cup of coffee was found still warm on the counter. The burglar {{9}} left just before the police arrived. They {{10}} been inside the shop for at least an hour.

When Mia arrived at the party two hours late, her friends had already left. She tried calling them but none of them answered – they {{1}} turned off their phones. She walked to the restaurant across the road and saw an empty table with dirty plates – her friends {{2}} had dinner there without her. A napkin with her name on it was left on a chair, so they {{3}} waited for a while before giving up. One friend had sent a message saying 'We're leaving' – but Mia {{4}} seen it in time because her phone had no signal. The others {{5}} been very confused about where she was. She {{6}} warned them about the signal problem earlier. When she finally reached them, they said they {{7}} been more worried if they hadn't seen her online earlier. They told her she {{8}} let them know sooner. The evening {{9}} gone very differently if Mia had left earlier. It was a lesson she {{10}} needed only once.

Archaeologists discovered the ruins of an ancient settlement in the jungle. The people {{1}} lived there for several centuries, based on the depth of the remains. The tools found were extremely sophisticated, which meant the inhabitants {{2}} been highly skilled craftspeople. Some walls were still standing, so the builders {{3}} used an advanced technique. Bones in the cooking area suggested the people {{4}} eaten a varied diet including fish and grains. No weapons were found, so the community {{5}} been peaceful. However, the settlement was abandoned very suddenly – food was still in storage jars – so something catastrophic {{6}} happened without warning. A layer of ash in the soil indicated there {{7}} been a volcanic eruption nearby. Survivors {{8}} fled to higher ground. Some objects resembled artefacts found in distant regions, suggesting traders {{9}} visited the settlement regularly. This discovery {{10}} changed everything scholars previously believed about this period of history.

The detective looked around the empty flat. The bed was made, the clothes were neatly folded, and there was a half-eaten breakfast on the table. The occupant {{1}} left in a rush. A suitcase was missing from the wardrobe – she {{2}} packed a bag before going. The cat's bowl was full and freshly filled, which meant she {{3}} been at home earlier that morning. Her passport was gone, so she {{4}} planned to travel abroad. But her concert ticket for the following evening was still on the table – she {{5}} known she would not return in time. Her phone was switched off; she {{6}} wanted to be contacted. No note had been left – the detective thought she {{7}} been frightened to write anything down. A neighbour said she had seemed nervous for days. Someone {{8}} been following or threatening her. The detective thought she {{9}} asked the police for help sooner. Whatever had happened, she {{10}} felt she had no other choice but to leave.

When the explorer returned from a six-month Arctic expedition, reporters were curious about what had gone wrong. His original team of eight {{1}} encountered extreme conditions almost immediately. Two members {{2}} suffered from frostbite in the first week, as both were treated on their return. The group's communication device {{3}} failed early on because they were out of contact for weeks. At one point, the team {{4}} been close to giving up – their supplies were critically low. However, they discovered a supply cache left by a previous team, which they {{5}} survived without. Whoever left that cache {{6}} had any idea it would save lives fifty years later. Some team members {{7}} experienced moments of real despair, but they pushed through. The youngest member, aged only twenty-two, {{8}} been incredibly brave. The explorer concluded that without teamwork, none of them {{9}} made it back alive. It {{10}} been the most intense experience of his life.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một tình huống bí ẩn mà bạn đã gặp – ví dụ: đồ vật biến mất, ai đó hành xử kỳ lạ, hoặc một sự kiện khó giải thích. Sử dụng suy luận quá khứ để đưa ra các khả năng.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must have, can't have, might have, could have, may have + past participle
writing_prompts.example: Last week, I came home to find my front door wide open, even though I was certain I had locked it before leaving. My first thought was that someone must have broken in, but nothing had been taken. I checked every room carefully. My spare key was missing from the kitchen drawer – someone must have known exactly where to look. I thought my flatmate might have come home early and forgotten to close the door, but she was abroad. It couldn't have been a stranger because nothing was disturbed. Then I remembered that my neighbour had mentioned needing to check on the plants – she might have let herself in and simply forgotten to lock up when she left. I called her and she apologised immediately. She must have been in a rush and simply walked out without pulling the door shut. I was relieved, but I realised I could have avoided hours of anxiety if I had just called her first.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một tình huống khi bạn hiểu nhầm ai đó hoặc phán xét sai. Nhìn lại, điều gì có thể đã thực sự xảy ra?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must have, might have, can't have + reflection on past misunderstanding
writing_prompts.example: A few months ago, I saw my colleague leave the office early without saying anything and assumed he must have been avoiding a difficult conversation we were supposed to have that afternoon. I spent the rest of the day feeling anxious and slightly offended. When I mentioned it to a mutual friend, she told me he had received an emergency call about a family member. He can't have been ignoring me at all – he must have been too distressed to stop and explain. I could have sent him a simple message asking if everything was alright, but instead I convinced myself he must have been upset with me. Looking back, I can see that I might have been projecting my own anxiety onto the situation. There must have been dozens of explanations I didn't consider. It was a reminder that we can't always know what someone is going through.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn là thám tử điều tra một vụ trộm tại bảo tàng. Dựa trên bằng chứng: cửa sổ vỡ, dấu chân trong bụi, camera bị tắt, bức tranh biến mất, một chiếc chìa khóa lạ gần lối ra. Hãy giải thích những gì có thể đã xảy ra.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must have, can't have, might have, could have for deduction chains
writing_prompts.example: Based on the evidence at the museum, it is clear that this was a carefully planned operation. The thief must have entered through the broken window on the east side, as the broken glass was on the inside floor. They can't have come through the main entrance because the electronic lock was untouched. The camera must have been disabled remotely, which means someone with technical knowledge must have been involved – this can't have been an amateur. The muddy footprints suggest there might have been two people, as the prints indicate different shoe sizes moving in opposite directions. The strange key found near the exit could have belonged to a former employee who still had access to certain corridors. The painting that was taken is extremely heavy, so one person couldn't have carried it alone – they must have had a vehicle waiting nearby. Someone might have tipped them off about the security schedule. This must have been planned for weeks.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một tình huống khi bạn phát hiện ra rằng một người bạn hoặc đồng nghiệp đang gặp khó khăn nhưng bạn không nhận ra trước đó. Dựa trên các dấu hiệu, bạn suy luận điều gì đã xảy ra?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must have, might have, can't have for interpreting behaviour and situations
hint_words: he must have been going through
writing_prompts.example: A few months ago, I noticed that my closest friend at work had been behaving strangely for several weeks. She must have been dealing with something serious because she stopped joining us for lunch and seemed withdrawn during meetings. She couldn't have been her usual cheerful self – there must have been something bothering her deeply. When I finally asked her about it, she told me she had been going through a difficult divorce and had been trying to handle everything alone. I felt terrible that I hadn't noticed sooner. She must have been carrying that burden for months without telling anyone. I realised there must have been signs I had missed – the tired eyes, the excuse about being busy, the way she avoided personal conversations. We can't always see what people are going through, but we must have been more attentive to our friends and colleagues. Looking back, I think she might have needed someone to ask directly, but she couldn't bring herself to reach out. It was a lesson that being present for others requires more than just physical presence.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một trường hợp khi bạn hoặc ai đó bị hiểu nhầm là đã làm điều gì đó sai, nhưng cuối cùng bằng chứng cho thấy họ vô tội. Sử dụng các cấu trúc suy luận quá khứ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: can't have, must have, might have, couldn't have in explaining misunderstandings
hint_words: there must have been a mistake
I must have jumped to conclusions
the facts must have been misinterpreted
writing_prompts.example: Last month, my neighbour accused me of accidentally damaging her car parked in front of my house. She said a large scratch appeared overnight and assumed I must have bumped it while parking my own car. I was confused because I was absolutely certain I couldn't have caused that scratch – my car is a different colour and shape, and the damage couldn't have come from my vehicle. She must have jumped to conclusions without considering other possibilities. A few days later, the security camera footage from the building across the street showed a delivery truck backing up into her car at three in the morning. The driver couldn't have known he had hit anything, and he simply drove away. When my neighbour saw the footage, she apologised profusely. She said she should have waited for more evidence before accusing me. I told her that misunderstandings happen, but she must have felt terrible about it for days. This incident taught me that we should never assume we know the full story – there must have been logical explanations for everything if we just looked carefully enough.

upper_intermediate.title: Likely, Unlikely, Bound, Definitely, Probably: Probability
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Likely / Unlikely – Tính từ diễn đạt xác suất
likely = có khả năng xảy ra  |  unlikely = ít có khả năng xảy ra

Cấu trúc 1: be + likely/unlikely + to-infinitive
She is likely to pass the exam.
It is unlikely to rain today.
He is unlikely to agree with the plan.

Cấu trúc 2: It is likely/unlikely + that + clause
It is likely that she will pass the exam.
It is unlikely that it will rain today.
📌 Bound / Sure – Chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra
bound to và sure to diễn đạt sự chắc chắn cao về tương lai:
She's bound to succeed – she's incredibly talented.
The match is sure to be exciting.
There are bound to be some problems at the start.

⚠️ Cấu trúc: be bound/sure + to + infinitive
⚠️ Cũng dùng: there is/are bound/sure to be + noun
📌 Definitely / Probably – Trạng từ diễn đạt mức độ chắc chắn
Vị trí trong câu khẳng định:
→ Trước động từ chính, SAU trợ động từ (nếu có):
He definitely knows the answer.
She will definitely get the job.
They are probably at home.
He has probably already left.

Vị trí trong câu phủ định:
→ Trước trợ động từ (với dạng rút gọn):
They probably won't come. ✔
She definitely isn't telling the truth. ✔

→ Sau trợ động từ (không dùng dạng rút gọn):
They will probably not come. ✔
She is definitely not telling the truth. ✔
📊 Thang mức độ xác suất (từ thấp đến cao)
Biểu đạtMức độ
unlikely to / unlikely thatÍt có khả năng (~20%)
probably won'tCó lẽ không (~30–40%)
probably will / likely toCó lẽ (~60–70%)
bound/sure toGần như chắc chắn (~90%)
definitelyChắc chắn hoàn toàn (100%)
She is likely to win – she has trained harder than anyone else.likely + to-infinitive → có khả năng cao
They will definitely find out the truth sooner or later.definitely sau trợ động từ will
There are bound to be delays at the border during peak season.There are bound to be → chắc chắn sẽ có

exercises.q: She's trained for months. She's ___ to win the competition.
exercises.exp: Tập luyện nhiều tháng → gần như chắc chắn → bound to win

exercises.q: He ___ won't pass the exam – he hasn't opened a single book.
exercises.exp: probably won't = câu phủ định → trạng từ đứng TRƯỚC won't

exercises.q: It is ___ that the project will be finished on time. We're already three weeks behind.
exercises.exp: Ba tuần trễ → ít có khả năng hoàn thành đúng hạn → unlikely that

exercises.q: There are ___ to be some problems when the new system launches.
exercises.exp: There are bound to be = chắc chắn sẽ có vấn đề

exercises.q: She ___ knows about the surprise – she was in the room when we planned it.
exercises.exp: Có mặt khi lên kế hoạch → chắc chắn biết → definitely

exercises.q: The weather forecast looks terrible. It's ___ to rain all weekend.
exercises.exp: Dự báo xấu → có khả năng mưa → likely to rain

exercises.q: He's been preparing for this presentation all month. It's ___ to be excellent.
exercises.exp: Chuẩn bị kỹ suốt một tháng → gần như chắc chắn → bound to be

exercises.q: She ___ isn't coming – she told me she had plans this evening.
exercises.exp: Đã có kế hoạch → chắc chắn không đến → definitely isn't

exercises.q: It's ___ that they'll agree to our proposal. They've always been very cooperative.
exercises.exp: Luôn hợp tác tốt → có khả năng đồng ý → likely that

exercises.q: The company is losing money. They are ___ to reduce their workforce soon.
exercises.exp: Đang thua lỗ → có khả năng cắt giảm nhân sự → likely to

exercises.q: With those qualifications, she's ___ to get the job. She's the strongest candidate.
exercises.exp: Ứng viên mạnh nhất → gần như chắc chắn → bound to get

exercises.q: He ___ forgot to set his alarm – that's why he missed the meeting.
exercises.exp: Suy đoán dựa trên thói quen → probably (trước động từ chính)

exercises.q: It is ___ that she'll change her mind. She's extremely stubborn.
exercises.exp: Rất cứng đầu → ít có khả năng thay đổi → unlikely that

exercises.q: Traffic on the motorway is always bad on Fridays. There's ___ to be a jam.
exercises.exp: Luôn tắc đường thứ Sáu → gần như chắc chắn có tắc đường → bound to be

exercises.q: He will ___ be promoted. He has the best performance record in the team.
exercises.exp: Thành tích tốt nhất → chắc chắn được thăng → definitely (sau will)

exercises.q: The flight ___ won't be delayed – the weather is perfect today.
exercises.exp: probably won't = có lẽ không (trước won't)

exercises.q: It's ___ to snow in July in Vietnam. The climate is tropical.
exercises.exp: Khí hậu nhiệt đới → không thể có tuyết tháng 7 → unlikely to

exercises.q: She has ___ already heard the announcement. It was on the news this morning.
exercises.exp: Đã phát trên tin tức → có lẽ đã nghe → probably (sau has)

exercises.q: ___ there are going to be questions about the new policy at the meeting.
exercises.opts: There are bound to be,It is definitely,Probably,Unlikely that
exercises.exp: Chắc chắn sẽ có câu hỏi → There are bound to be questions

exercises.q: They've never lost a match this season. They're ___ to win the championship.
exercises.exp: Không thua trận nào → có khả năng cao vô địch → likely to win

exercises.q: With all that experience, she is ___ get the promotion. (almost certain)
exercises.exp: Kinh nghiệm dày dặn → gần như chắc chắn → bound to

exercises.q: He ___ finish the project before the deadline. He's already behind. (probably / negative)
exercises.exp: probably won't = câu phủ định với probably đứng trước won't

exercises.q: It is ___ the new law will be passed next month. (likely / that)
exercises.exp: It is likely that + clause

exercises.q: There are ___ long queues at the border during the holidays. (certain)
exercises.exp: There are bound to be + noun

exercises.q: She ___ won't agree. She has refused every similar proposal before. (certain / negative)
exercises.exp: definitely won't = chắc chắn không → definitely đứng trước won't

exercises.q: The economy is growing quickly. It is ___ that unemployment will fall. (likely)
exercises.exp: Kinh tế tăng trưởng → có khả năng thất nghiệp giảm → likely that

exercises.q: He's been training for a year. He's ___ break the record. (almost certain)
exercises.exp: Tập luyện một năm → gần như chắc chắn → bound to break

exercises.q: The match ___ starts at seven, but let me double-check. (perhaps)
exercises.exp: probably starts = trạng từ trước động từ chính

exercises.q: It is ___ that he will change his mind at the last minute. (not likely)
exercises.exp: unlikely that = not likely that

exercises.q: She will ___ win the debate. She is the most prepared. (certainly)
exercises.exp: definitely sau will = chắc chắn

exercises.q: Given the evidence, it is ___ that he was involved in the incident. (possible/probable)
exercises.exp: It is likely that + clause = có khả năng

exercises.q: The company is struggling financially. It is ___ to survive another year without major changes. (unlikely)
exercises.exp: Công ty gặp khó khăn → ít có khả năng tồn tại

exercises.q: He's a talented musician. He is ___ to become famous one day. (very likely)
exercises.exp: Tài năng → có khả năng nổi tiếng

exercises.q: The traffic is always terrible at this time. There are ___ to be delays. (certain)
exercises.exp: Luôn kẹt xe → chắc chắn có trì hoãn

exercises.q: I ___ won't be able to attend the meeting. I have a prior commitment. (certain)
exercises.exp: definitely won't = chắc chắn không thể tham dự

exercises.q: It is ___ that the new policy will reduce pollution. Environmental groups are skeptical. (unlikely)

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn nghĩ cuộc sống của bạn sẽ như thế nào trong 10 năm tới? Hãy dùng các biểu đạt xác suất để mô tả những gì bạn chắc chắn, có thể, hoặc ít có khả năng xảy ra.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: likely to, unlikely to, bound to, definitely, probably, it is likely that
writing_prompts.example: In ten years' time, I'm likely to be working in a completely different field from the one I'm in now, as technology is changing careers rapidly. I'll definitely have improved my language skills, since that's a goal I'm committed to. I'm bound to have faced a few setbacks along the way – everyone does – but I think they will probably make me stronger. It's unlikely that I'll still be living in the same city, as I'm already thinking about moving abroad. There are bound to be opportunities I haven't even imagined yet. I'm probably going to continue studying, as I believe learning never stops. One thing I'm definitely not likely to do is give up on my ambitions. I may well be running my own small business in a decade, though it's unlikely to be straightforward. Whatever happens, I'm bound to look back on this period as a turning point.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn phân tích về một vấn đề hoặc xu hướng trong xã hội hiện đại – ví dụ: AI và việc làm, mạng xã hội và sức khỏe tâm thần, hoặc biến đổi khí hậu. Sử dụng các biểu đạt xác suất để thể hiện quan điểm.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: likely, unlikely, bound, definitely, probably in analytical writing
writing_prompts.example: Artificial intelligence is likely to transform the job market more profoundly than any previous technological revolution. It is almost certain that certain routine jobs will disappear within a generation – tasks that currently require human effort are bound to be automated as software becomes more sophisticated. Some experts are definitely right to warn that governments will probably not act quickly enough to manage this transition. It is unlikely that all displaced workers will easily find equivalent employment without significant retraining programmes. There are bound to be social tensions if the benefits of AI are concentrated among a small number of corporations and investors. However, it is likely that new types of work will emerge that we cannot yet anticipate. History suggests that societies adapt, though the process is probably going to be painful and uneven. One thing is definitely certain: the way we define work and value human contribution is bound to change fundamentally in the coming decades.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy dự đoán điều gì sẽ xảy ra tại một sự kiện sắp tới mà bạn sẽ tham gia hoặc theo dõi – ví dụ: một kỳ thi, một buổi phỏng vấn, một trận đấu thể thao, hay một sự kiện gia đình.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: bound to, likely to, probably, definitely, unlikely to for predictions
writing_prompts.example: My sister is getting married next month, and I'm already thinking about what the day will probably be like. She has been planning everything for over a year, so it's bound to be beautifully organised. The weather in that season is likely to be warm and clear, though there's always a chance it might rain – there are bound to be a few clouds on the horizon. The food is definitely going to be delicious, as she has hired the best caterer in the area. The speeches are likely to be emotional – my father is bound to cry, and I'm probably going to cry too. Some guests from abroad will probably not arrive until the morning of the ceremony, which is unlikely to cause too many problems since the venue is flexible. There's bound to be dancing late into the night. It will definitely be one of the most memorable days our family has ever shared.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy phân tích một bộ phim, chương trình TV, hoặc cuốn sách mà bạn nghĩ sẽ rất phổ biến trong tương lai. Sử dụng các biểu đạt xác suất để giải thích tại sao nó sẽ thành công hoặc không.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: likely to, bound to, probably, definitely, unlikely to for analysis
writing_prompts.example: I believe this new streaming series about a family-run restaurant is likely to become one of the most popular shows of the decade. It is bound to attract a wide audience because it combines everything viewers love – great food, family drama, and stunning locations. The producers are probably going to create multiple seasons because the concept is flexible enough to develop endlessly. There are definitely going to be celebrity chef cameos, which will probably attract even casual viewers who might not otherwise watch food-related content. Critics are unlikely to appreciate the simplicity of the storyline, but they probably will in terms of viewership numbers. It is likely that the show will inspire copycat productions from other networks as well, since there are bound to be similar formats following in its wake. One thing I am definitely sure about is that the actors are going to become household names within a year.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một người bạn hoặc thành viên gia đình mà bạn nghĩ sẽ thay đổi nhiều nhất trong 5 năm tới. Bạn dự đoán điều gì, và tại sao bạn nghĩ như vậy?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: bound to, likely to, probably, definitely, unlikely to for predictions about people
writing_prompts.example: My younger brother is likely to become a completely different person by the time he turns thirty. He is bound to travel the world first – he has always been curious about other cultures – and I think that experience will probably change his perspective on everything from career to relationships. He is unlikely to follow the traditional path our parents expected of him, since he has always been independent and unconventional. He will definitely face some difficult moments along the way, but there are bound to be breakthrough moments too. He is probably going to settle down somewhere unexpected, possibly abroad, and I think he will probably marry someone from a completely different background than ours. One thing I am definitely sure about is that he is unlikely to ever lose his sense of adventure. He is bound to keep surprising us all.

upper_intermediate.title: Used to, Be Used to, Get Used to
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Used to + infinitive – Thói quen/trạng thái trong QUÁ KHỨ (không còn đúng nữa)
Diễn đạt những gì đã từng làm hoặc từng đúng trong quá khứ, nhưng hiện tại KHÔNG còn như vậy:
I used to smoke, but I quit five years ago.
She used to live in the countryside when she was a child.
We used to have a dog. (= We don't have one now.)

⚠️ Phủ định: didn't use to
I didn't use to like vegetables, but now I love them.
⚠️ Câu hỏi: Did you use to...?
Did you use to play sports at school?

⚠️ KHÔNG có dạng hiện tại của "used to" → Dùng usually + present simple để nói về thói quen hiện tại:
We usually eat out on Saturdays. ✔ (NOT: We use to eat out...)
📌 Be used to + noun / -ing – Đã quen với điều gì đó
Diễn đạt rằng điều gì đó không còn lạ lẫm hay khó khăn nữa với bạn:
I am used to waking up early. (= Tôi đã quen dậy sớm.)
She is used to the noise in the city. (= Tiếng ồn không làm phiền cô ấy nữa.)
He was used to working long hours.

⚠️ Sau be used to, dùng noun hoặc verb-ing, KHÔNG dùng bare infinitive:
She is used to driving on the left. ✔
She is used to drive on the left. ✘

⚠️ Chia theo thì: am/is/are/was/were used to
📌 Get used to + noun / -ing – Dần dần quen với điều gì đó
Diễn đạt quá trình trở nên quen dần với điều gì đó mới hoặc khó khăn:
I'm getting used to the cold weather here.
She found it hard at first, but she got used to living alone.
You'll get used to the new system eventually.

⚠️ Sau get used to, cũng dùng noun hoặc verb-ing
⚠️ So sánh 3 cấu trúc
Cấu trúcÝ nghĩaTheo sau bởi
used to doThói quen/trạng thái QK (không còn)bare infinitive
be used to doingĐã quen với (hiện tại/quá khứ)noun / verb-ing
get used to doingĐang dần quen (quá trình)noun / verb-ing
I used to be very shy, but I'm much more confident now.Trạng thái QK không còn đúng → used to + bare infinitive
She is used to working under pressure. It doesn't bother her at all.Đã quen rồi → be used to + -ing
He is getting used to the new commute. It was hard at first.Đang dần quen → get used to + -ing

The grandmother, who {{7}} be afraid of dogs, discovered that Bruno was gentle and calm. By the end of the first month, she {{8}} having him sit at her feet while she read. The whole family agrees that they {{9}} life without Bruno now. They say they {{10}} to thinking of him as a full member of the family – and they wouldn't have it any other way.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một thói quen hoặc cách sống từ quá khứ mà bạn không còn duy trì nữa. Tại sao nó thay đổi, và bạn cảm thấy thế nào về sự thay đổi đó?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to + infinitive, didn't use to, contrasted with present habits
writing_prompts.example: When I was at secondary school, I used to read for at least an hour every evening before bed. I didn't use to own a smartphone, so there was nothing to distract me from books. I used to visit the local library every Saturday and come home with a stack of novels. I also used to write in a diary every night, which helped me process my thoughts. All of that changed when I started university and got my first smartphone. Gradually, I stopped reading before bed and started scrolling through social media instead. I used to find it easy to concentrate for long periods, but now I struggle to read more than a few pages without checking my phone. I miss those quieter evenings, and I've been trying to rebuild the habit. I didn't use to need any effort to read – it was simply something I did naturally. Recovering that habit has been harder than I expected, but I'm slowly getting there.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn hoặc ai đó bạn biết đã từng thay đổi môi trường sống hay làm việc đáng kể – chuyển thành phố, đi du học, bắt đầu công việc mới. Hãy mô tả quá trình thích nghi, những thứ đã quen và những thứ vẫn còn khó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to, be used to, get used to + contrast of adaptation stages
writing_prompts.example: When I moved from a small town to Ho Chi Minh City for work three years ago, almost everything felt overwhelming. I used to live in a place where the streets were quiet after eight in the evening, so I wasn't used to the constant noise and traffic at all hours. I didn't use to eat street food regularly, but it quickly became a daily pleasure. Getting used to the city's pace was the hardest part – I was used to a much slower rhythm of life. For the first few months, I got lost on my motorbike almost every day because I wasn't used to the one-way streets and sudden turns. I gradually got used to navigating without a map. I'm still getting used to the heat, though – even after three years. I used to think I was adaptable, but this experience has taught me that adapting takes real time and genuine effort. Now I am used to city life and actually love it.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về một khía cạnh của cuộc sống hiện đại mà mọi người đã phải học cách thích nghi trong những thập kỷ gần đây – ví dụ: làm việc từ xa, sử dụng công nghệ số, hoặc lối sống đô thị.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to + infinitive (the past), be used to / get used to (current state of adaptation)
hint_words: many are still getting used to
society has had to get used to
writing_prompts.example: Before the pandemic, most office workers didn't use to think seriously about remote working. They used to commute every day, sit at a fixed desk, and attend meetings in person. Very few people were used to managing their time entirely from home, and many companies weren't used to trusting employees without direct supervision. When everything changed in 2020, people suddenly had to get used to video calls, home offices, and the blurring of work and personal life. Some adapted quickly – those who used to freelance or travel for work were already used to working independently. Others found it genuinely difficult. Many workers are now completely used to remote or hybrid arrangements and would not go back. However, there are those who still haven't fully got used to the isolation that comes with working from home. Society has had to get used to a new definition of what a working day looks like – and the adjustment is still ongoing.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về kỷ niệm tuổi thơ với ông bà hoặc người thân lớn tuổi. Mô tả những thói quen hoặc hoạt động mà họ thường làm, những câu chuyện họ kể, và cách những ký ức đó vẫn ảnh hưởng đến bạn.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to, would for past habits and routines of older generations
hint_words: I still remember when she used to
writing_prompts.example: Every summer when I was a child, my grandmother used to prepare the most wonderful meals for the whole family. She would wake up before dawn to start cooking, and the smell of her special soup would fill the entire house by the time we came downstairs. My grandfather would sit in the garden every morning, drinking tea and reading the newspaper, and he would tell us stories about what life was like when he was young. We used to spend hours listening to his tales – he would describe walking to school in the rain, or how the village would gather every evening to share news and songs. My grandmother didn't use to understand why we spent so much time on our phones, but she would smile and say she was happy we still visited. I didn't use to appreciate those moments as much as I should have. Now that they are both gone, I realise those summer afternoons with them were truly precious. I would give anything to have those mornings back.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả những thay đổi trong thói quen sinh hoạt của một người bạn hoặc thành viên gia đình qua nhiều năm. Sử dụng used to và would để so sánh quá khứ và hiện tại.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to vs would with specific habits and states, contrast with present
writing_prompts.example: My older brother used to be the most organised person I knew. Every morning, he would make his bed before leaving for school, and he would always finish his homework at least a day before it was due. He used to wake up at six every day without an alarm, and he would go for a run before breakfast. In the evenings, he would read for at least an hour before sleeping. He didn't use to eat junk food at all – he would always choose fruits and vegetables over snacks. All of that changed when he went to university. He no longer follows the same strict routine, and he didn't use to expect that life would become so different. Now he stays up late, rarely exercises, and eats whatever is convenient. He admits that he used to be much healthier but doesn't regret the change because university life demands flexibility. Sometimes he wishes he could return to those disciplined days, but he knows he would have to make significant changes to get there.

upper_intermediate.title: Would and Used to: Past Habits
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Used to – Dùng khi nào?
1. Thói quen lặp đi lặp lại trong quá khứ (không còn nữa):
When I was a child, I used to go skiing every winter.
From the age of 12 till I was 16, I used to dance at weekends.

2. Trạng thái/tình huống quá khứ (không còn đúng):
As a child, I used to be blond.
I used to live with my mum when I was a child.

⚠️ Lưu ý: Không có thì hiện tại của "used to"
❌ I use to live in London. → ✅ I usually live in London.
📌 Would – Dùng khi nào?
Thói quen lặp đi lặp lại trong quá khứ (hành động):
When I was a child, we would go to the shopping centre every Saturday.
Every summer, he would visit his grandparents in the countryside.

⚠️ Lưu ý quan trọng: KHÔNG dùng "would" cho trạng thái!
❌ I would be nervous as a child. → ✅ I used to be nervous.
❌ She would have long hair. → ✅ She used to have long hair.
📐 So sánh Used to vs Would
Đặc điểmUsed toWould
Thói quen lặp QK✅✅
Trạng thái QK✅❌
Câu phủ địnhdidn't use towouldn't
Câu hỏiDid you use to...?Would you...?
When I was young, I used to be very shy and never spoke in public.Trạng thái → chỉ dùng used to
Every Sunday, my grandfather would sit in his armchair and read the newspaper.Hành động lặp → dùng would hoặc used to

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một thói quen hoặc sở thích của bạn thời trẻ mà bây giờ không còn nữa – bạn đã làm gì, bao lâu, và tại sao bạn không làm nữa
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Used to for past habits and states, would for repeated past actions
writing_prompts.example: When I was young, I used to collect stamps from all over the world. I would spend every Saturday morning at the antique market looking for rare stamps from different countries. I used to have hundreds of albums filled with my collection, and I would carefully organise each stamp by country and date. My father used to travel for work, and he would always bring me stamps from his trips. I used to dream about becoming a professional philatelist one day. However, as I grew older, I stopped collecting stamps because I didn't have enough time anymore. Now I keep the albums in my study, and sometimes I still look through them and remember those happy days.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả cuộc sống của một người bạn hoặc thành viên trong gia đình trước đây – họ đã sống như thế nào, làm gì, và điều gì đã thay đổi
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Used to for past situations, would for habitual past actions, stative verbs
writing_prompts.example: My grandmother used to live in a small village where everyone knew each other. She would wake up before sunrise every morning and work in the vegetable garden until noon. She used to say that fresh air and hard work were the secrets to a happy life. They didn't use to have electricity in her village, so she would cook on an open fire and read by candlelight. She used to make her own clothes and would teach my mother how to sew. Now she lives with us in the city, but she still misses those simple times when life was slower and neighbours were like family. She says that modern life is too fast and complicated.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một chuyến đi hoặc kỳ nghỉ đáng nhớ trong quá khứ – bạn đã làm gì, ở đâu, và tại sao nó đặc biệt với bạn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Past Simple for specific events, used to/would for habitual activities during the trip
writing_prompts.example: When I was twelve, my family and I used to spend every summer at my uncle's beach house. We would wake up early and spend the whole day swimming and building sandcastles on the beach. Every afternoon, my cousins would come to visit and we would play games until sunset. I used to love those summer holidays because there were no schedules and we could do whatever we wanted. We didn't use to worry about homework or responsibilities during those weeks. The best part was the midnight bonfires where we would tell ghost stories and roast marshmallows. Those memories still make me smile whenever I think about them.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một thay đổi lớn trong cuộc sống của bạn – trước đây bạn đã sống/như thế nào và bây giờ khác nhau thế nào
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Used to for past states and habits, contrast with present habits
writing_prompts.example: My life has changed dramatically over the past decade. I used to be very shy and would avoid social situations whenever possible. I used to think that I was an introvert who would never enjoy meeting new people. I would spend most of my time alone reading books and watching movies. I didn't use to care about my appearance or fitness, and I would eat whatever I wanted without thinking about health. However, after starting university, I decided to change. I would join clubs and attend social events even when I felt nervous. Now I am a completely different person who loves networking and travelling. My life used to be simple and predictable, but now it is exciting and full of new challenges every day.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một người hàng xóm hoặc người bạn cũ mà bạn đã không gặp trong nhiều năm – họ đã thay đổi như thế nào từ khi bạn còn nhỏ
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Used to for past characteristics and habits, description of changes over time
writing_prompts.example: I remember my childhood neighbour Minh who used to live next door to us for fifteen years. When we were young, he would always come to my house after school to play video games and do homework together. He used to be the tallest boy in our class and would help everyone who was being bullied. We used to spend every weekend riding bicycles around the neighbourhood until our parents called us home for dinner. He used to dream about becoming a doctor and would read medical books even during summer vacation. Unfortunately, his family moved away ten years ago and I haven't seen him since. I heard that he did become a doctor and now works at a hospital in another city. Sometimes I wonder if he still remembers our adventures and the promises we used to make to stay friends forever.

upper_intermediate.title: Verbs of the Senses
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Năm động từ giác quan
Look, smell, taste, sound, feel – Đây là các động từ chỉ giác quan, dùng để mô tả cách mà mọi thứ được cảm nhận qua các giác quan.
📌 Cấu trúc 1: Động từ giác quan + Tính từ
Chủ ngữ + look/smell/taste/sound/feel + tính từ
The music sounds beautiful.
She looks tired today – maybe she didn't sleep well.
This coffee tastes too sweet. Can I have some sugar?
The roses smell wonderful in the garden.
His idea sounds interesting – let's discuss it further.
After the massage, I feel relaxed and refreshed.
📌 Cấu trúc 2: Động từ giác quan + Like + Danh từ
Chủ ngữ + feel/smell/taste/sound/look + like + danh từ
You sound like your mother when you talk.
This tastes like chicken, but I'm not sure what it actually is.
The fabric feels like silk – it's so smooth!
She looks like a movie star with that dress.
His excuse sounds like a lie to me.
📌 Cấu trúc 3: Động từ giác quan + As if/As though + Mệnh đề
Chủ ngữ + feel/smell/taste/sound/look + as if/as though + S + V
You sound as if you had a long party last night. (= bạn nghe như thể...)
You look as though you've just seen a ghost.
She feels as if the world is falling apart.

⚠️ Trong tiếng Anh thân mật, ta thường dùng like thay cho as if/as though:
You sound like you had a long party last night.
📐 Lưu ý quan trọng
Động từ giác quan (khi dùng với nghĩa mô tả) thuộc nhóm stative verbs → KHÔNG dùng với -ing form trong ngữ cảnh này!
❌ She is looking beautifully. → ✅ She looks beautiful.
❌ The music is sounding strange. → ✅ The music sounds strange.
The plan sounds like a good idea to me.Từ giác quan + like + danh từ → mô tả sự so sánh
You look as if you haven't slept in days.Từ giác quan + as if + mệnh đề → dự đoán kết quả
This soup tastes absolutely delicious.Từ giác quan + tính từ → mô tả trực tiếp

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả trải nghiệm ăn uống đáng nhớ của bạn – món ăn trông, ngửi, nếm và cảm giác như thế nào khi bạn thưởng thức nó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Verbs of the senses: looks like, smells like, tastes, feels, sounds as if
writing_prompts.example: Last summer, I had the most incredible dining experience at a traditional Japanese restaurant in my city. The moment I entered, the interior looked like a peaceful temple with wooden panels and soft ambient lighting. The air smelled like fresh wasabi and grilled fish, which made my stomach growl immediately. When the chef served the sashimi, each piece looked like a small piece of art carefully arranged on the plate. The salmon tasted incredibly fresh, melting in my mouth like butter. The texture felt smooth and silky, nothing like the frozen versions I had tried before. My friend said the sake tasted as if it had been aged for decades, with a complex flavour that developed on the tongue. The whole experience sounded like a symphony of flavours dancing together in perfect harmony, and I left feeling completely satisfied and already planning my next visit.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một giấc mơ hoặc tưởng tượng kỳ lạ – bạn cảm thấy, nghe thấy, hoặc nhìn thấy điều gì và nó giống như gì
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Verbs of the senses in hypothetical/as if contexts
writing_prompts.example: Last night I had one of the strangest dreams I have ever experienced. In the dream, I found myself walking through a forest that looked like it existed in another dimension. The trees were made of crystal and reflected rainbow colours everywhere, making everything look like a painting come to life. I could hear water sounds as if it were singing a melody, and the wind felt like gentle hands guiding me forward. Suddenly, I met a creature that looked like a mixture between a deer and a bird, with feathers instead of fur. It spoke to me, and its voice sounded as though it came from deep underground, ancient and wise. The whole experience felt like a journey into another world where the normal rules of nature did not apply. When I woke up, my room felt tiny and ordinary compared to the magical universe I had just left behind.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả cảm giác sau một trải nghiệm mạo hiểm (nhảy dù, lặn,...) – mọi giác quan hoạt động như thế nào
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: All five senses: look, sound, smell, taste, feel in vivid description
writing_prompts.example: The moment I jumped out of the plane at 15,000 feet, every sense in my body came alive at once. The wind sounded like a roaring lion as it rushed past my ears at incredible speed. Below me, the landscape looked like a miniature model with tiny houses and green fields that seemed to belong to another world. The cold air smelled crisp and pure, nothing like the polluted city air I was used to breathing. My stomach felt as if it had dropped to my feet, and my heart was pounding so hard that I could feel it beating against my chest like a drum. When the parachute finally opened, everything went quiet suddenly, and the world felt peaceful and calm, like floating in a silent ocean of blue sky. The landing was gentle, and the grass smelled fresh and earthy. It was the most exhilarating experience of my life, one that I will never forget.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một không gian yêu thích của bạn (quán cà phê, thư viện, công viên) – nó trông, nghe, ngửi và cảm giác như thế nào
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Descriptive use of all five senses to create atmosphere
writing_prompts.example: My favourite corner in the city is a tiny bookshop café tucked away on a quiet side street. From the outside, the shop looks like a portal to another era, with old wooden shelves and a vintage sign that has been there for decades. Inside, the air smells like a mixture of old paper and freshly brewed coffee, a combination that feels like a warm hug for the soul. The soft jazz music sounds like it is playing from another room, barely audible but comforting. The worn leather chairs feel soft and inviting, as if they have held thousands of readers over the years. Every afternoon, sunlight streams through the dusty windows and everything looks golden and peaceful. The owner, an elderly lady, always greets customers with a smile that sounds as kind as her heart must be. It is my sanctuary where time seems to move slower and the outside world feels miles away.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một cơn bão hoặc hiện tượng thời tiết mạnh mẽ mà bạn đã trải qua – mọi thứ xung quanh thay đổi như thế nào
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Dynamic use of sensory verbs to describe dramatic weather changes
writing_prompts.example: Last autumn, I experienced the most terrifying storm of my life while staying at my grandparents' coastal house. The sky turned dark as night in the middle of the afternoon, looking like an angry painter had spilled black ink across the heavens. The thunder sounded like explosions from a war zone, making the windows shake violently with each strike. The rain fell so heavily that it felt like thousands of tiny needles attacking the roof all at once. The wind howled outside, sounding as if it were a wild animal trying to break through every opening. Everything in the house felt as if it was holding its breath, waiting for the storm to pass. The air smelled electric and metallic, charged with energy from the lightning that flashed constantly. My grandfather said the sea looked like a monster awakening, with waves crashing against the rocks like fists pounding the earth. When the storm finally subsided, the silence felt deafening, and the fresh post-storm air smelled absolutely wonderful.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một ngày chợ hoặc khu chợ địa phương mà bạn đã từng ghé thăm – âm thanh, mùi hương, hình ảnh và cảm giác chung khi bạn đi bộ qua các gian hàng
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Verbs of the senses to create a vivid marketplace atmosphere
writing_prompts.example: The morning market near my house is a sensory explosion that begins even before you enter. From the street, the air smells like a mixture of fresh herbs, grilled meat, and ripe tropical fruits all blending together. Everything looks like a vibrant painting come to life with stalls displaying mountains of colourful vegetables and spices that shine like jewels in the early sunlight. The vendors' voices sound like a continuous symphony, each one shouting prices and recommendations as if in a friendly competition with their neighbours. The ground feels damp under your feet where vendors have sprinkled water to keep the produce fresh. The sounds of bargaining voices and the clinking of coins feel as though you have stepped into a completely different world from the modern city outside. Every corner you turn reveals something new that catches your eye, and the smell of strong Vietnamese coffee from a nearby stall feels absolutely irresistible. The entire experience sounds and looks like a scene from a traditional Vietnamese painting, and I always leave feeling energised and connected to my roots.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một khoảnh khắc đặc biệt khi bạn nhận ra điều gì đó qua giác quan – ví dụ: nhận ra ai đó qua giọng nói, nhận ra mùi của nhà cũ, hoặc cảm nhận được cảm xúc qua ngoại hình
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Verbs of the senses in recognition and emotional understanding
writing_prompts.example: The moment my best friend walked into the restaurant, I knew something was wrong before she even sat down. She looked like her usual self on the surface, with her hair neatly styled and wearing a professional outfit, but there was something in the way she moved that felt as though she was carrying a heavy burden. Her voice sounded different – usually bright and animated, it now felt flat and reluctant, nothing like the cheerful person I had known for years. When she finally sat down, her eyes looked like they had been crying for hours, even though her face was composed. The food we ordered tasted like nothing because we were both distracted by the elephant in the room. My sixth sense told me that whatever had happened, it must have been serious enough to affect someone who always appeared so strong and confident. When she finally told me about her diagnosis, everything suddenly made sense – the way she had looked at me earlier, the nervous laughter that sounded hollow, and the way she had touched her face unconsciously as if checking if it still felt real. Some things cannot be hidden forever – they always make themselves known through the senses we trust most.

upper_intermediate.title: Do or Make: Which is it?
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Quy tắc cơ bản
Do = hành động không tạo ra vật thể có thể nhìn thấy được
Make = hành động tạo ra/sản xuất/kết quả có thể nhận thấy được
📌 Các cụm từ với DO
Cụm từVí dụ
Do homework/workI'll do my homework after dinner.
Do the dishes/laundry/shoppingShe does the laundry every Saturday.
Do a course/favourI'm doing a course in Spanish.
Do well/badlyHe did well in his exams.
Do damage/good/harmThe storm did a lot of damage.
📌 Các cụm từ với MAKE
Cụm từVí dụ
Make lunch/dinner/a cakeI'll make lunch for everyone.
Make a decision/choiceShe made a decision to quit.
Make a promise/mistakeHe made a promise to call.
Make money/a profitThey make money online.
Make friends/an apologyShe made friends quickly.
📌 Các ngoại lệ quan trọng!
❌ Do your bed → ✅ Make your bed (làm giường)
❌ Make the ironing → ✅ Do the ironing
⚠️ Học thuộc các collocations cố định!
I'll do the shopping and then make dinner.shopping = tác vụ; dinner = thực phẩm được tạo ra
He made a mistake, but she did her best to fix it.mistake = tạo ra; best = nỗ lực
They make a lot of money and do very little work.Tiền = tạo ra; công việc = tác vụ
